summary: Mark Zuckerberg shares his core hiring principle for building strong teams at Meta: only hire individuals you would be happy to work for in an alternate universe. This approach ensures the organization is filled with respected leaders rather than candidates who are merely 'good enough'.

principles.statement: Only hire someone if you'd be happy working for them in an alternate universe.
principles.rationale: It ensures you are bringing in people whose judgment, insight, and values you truly respect, which ultimately builds a company filled with leaders worth following.
principles.source_quote: only hire someone if you'd be happy working for them in an alternate universe.

mental_models.name: The Alternate Universe Hiring Test
mental_models.description: A lens for evaluating job candidates: imagine an alternate reality where the roles are reversed. If you would not be happy working for the candidate in that universe, you should not hire them in this one.
mental_models.example: Applying this mindset at every level of an organization to ensure the company is filled with leaders worth following.

heuristics: Never settle for 'good enough' when hiring; look for people whose judgment and values you truly respect.

signature_quotes.text: only hire someone if you'd be happy working for them in an alternate universe.
signature_quotes.context: Describing his primary hiring principle for building strong teams at Meta.

anti_patterns.description: Settling for candidates who are merely 'good enough'.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It compromises the quality of leadership and prevents the organization from being filled with people whose judgment, insight, and values are truly respected.
